seoTitle: How to Change n8n Timezone (Cloud & Self-Hosted)
metaDescription: Learn how to change your n8n timezone at the workflow level or via environment variables so scheduled triggers fire at the right time.
intro: If your scheduled triggers and cron jobs are firing at the wrong time, it's usually a timezone mismatch between your n8n instance and your actual location. This guide covers adjusting timezone settings for a single workflow as well as changing the default instance-wide via environment variables on self-hosted setups.

steps.heading: Open the workflow you need to adjust
steps.body: Load the specific automation project whose scheduling is affected.

steps.heading: Open workflow settings
steps.body: Access the settings icon to open the local parameter adjustment panel for that workflow.

steps.heading: Find the timezone dropdown
steps.body: Locate the timezone selection field and choose the correct zone for that workflow's triggers.

steps.heading: Check environment variables for global changes
steps.body: On self-hosted instances, you can also change the default timezone instance-wide through environment variable settings.

steps.heading: Save your changes
steps.body: Apply the new timezone mapping so it takes effect for the workflow.

steps.heading: Recalibrate cron or interval nodes
steps.body: Review any scheduled trigger nodes to make sure their timing still matches your intended schedule, then run a test to confirm.

faq.question: Can I set a different timezone per workflow?
faq.answer: Yes, individual workflow settings let you set a localized timezone that's separate from your instance's global default.

faq.question: How do I change the timezone for a self-hosted instance globally?
faq.answer: You typically adjust the relevant environment variable in your self-hosted setup to change the default zone for the entire instance.

faq.question: Why did my scheduled trigger fire at the wrong time?
faq.answer: This is almost always a timezone mismatch — double-check both the workflow's local timezone setting and your server's system clock.

faq.question: Do I need to update existing cron nodes after changing the timezone?
faq.answer: Yes, it's worth reviewing existing chronological trigger nodes to confirm their timing still lines up correctly after any timezone change.
